mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate, maintained by the central bank to combat persistent inflation, increases borrowing costs for corporations and compresses equity valuation multiples. This restrictive policy stance leads to negative forward returns as long as the policy rate remains above a critical threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has raised rates 10 times since March 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market and robust consumer health, which supports corporate earnings. As long as the unemployment rate remains at or near cyclical lows, it provides a positive tailwind for equity markets over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 3.4% is a multi-decade low
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive and significant (p < 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both broad consumer spending (retail sales) and housing investment (housing starts) indicates a widespread loss of demand momentum. This joint signal of weakness in two key economic sectors precedes earnings downgrades and leads to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when both retail sales and housing starts 3-month momentum are negative fails to generate a positive information ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects expanding output and healthy corporate activity. This growth in the economy's supply side translates directly into higher revenues and profits, driving positive equity market performance over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, any upside surprise in the monthly CPI print increases market expectations of a more hawkish central bank response. This repricing of the future path of interest rates causes an immediate negative reaction in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Current YoY CPI is 4.9% as of April 2023 data
failure_condition: During periods where YoY CPI is above 4%, the average 5-day S&P 500 return following a monthly CPI release that exceeds consensus expectations is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of excessively strong job growth and high inflation signals an overheating economy. This 'hot' state forces the central bank to tighten policy aggressively, outweighing the benefits of strong employment and leading to negative equity returns as financial conditions deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: 3-month avg PAYEMS change > 200k AND YoY CPIAUCSL > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:Recent labor market data has been unexpectedly strong despite high inflation
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive average 60-day return during historical periods when both strong payroll growth and high inflation conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in quarterly real GDP is the most direct signal of a recession. When this occurs, it confirms a severe slowdown in aggregate demand, triggering broad-based downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and causing negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following the official release of a negative quarterly real GDP growth number, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate is a primary input into the discount rate used for equity valuation. A higher rate mechanically increases the discount factor applied to future earnings, reducing their present value and putting continuous downward pressure on the S&P 500 index level.
failure_condition: The beta coefficient of 20-day forward S&P 500 returns on the level of the FEDFUNDS rate is non-negative in a rolling 5-year regression.
